issues_found: PUBLISHED_AT is blank in metadata, making it impossible to verify source recency through standard means
Content is thin (350 words) and primarily factual/operational rather than analytical
Source contains many navigation elements (FAQs, Program Terms, Approved Counterparties links) rather than substantive analysis
human_review_reason: The record is factually accurate and faithfully represents the source content. While the source is operationally-focused rather than analytically rich, it provides an authoritative institutional record of the Fed's pandemic-era agency CMBS program. The BlackRock vendor relationship and timeline are correctly documented. Given that this documents a specific historical Fed balance sheet operation during COVID-19, it has archival value even if the content is thin.

corrected_fields.macro_context: The Fed's agency CMBS purchase program (March 2020 – November 2021) was a notable expansion of the Fed's balance sheet toolkit during the COVID-19 pandemic, extending purchases beyond traditional Treasury and agency MBS into commercial mortgage-backed securities. This operation was temporary and specifically designed for crisis response, with purchases ceasing when market functioning stabilized.
